2606.17952.title: SoftMoE: Soft Differentiable Routing for Mixture-of-Experts in LLMs
2606.17952.paper_summary: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures enable scaling LLM parameters under a fixed inference budget by activating only a small subset of experts via top-$k$ routing. While this preserves causality and suits autoregressive language models, the discrete top-$k$ operator is not differentiable, forcing a fixed number of active experts per input and resulting in inefficient use of computation. We propose SoftMoE, which replaces discrete routing with a truncated soft top-$k$ LapSum relaxation, allowing gradient-based optimization of expert routing. We further parameterize the mean number of active experts per layer and impose a global budget constraint, enabling the model to learn how to allocate expert capacity across layers. SoftMoE remains fully compatible with autoregressive modeling and achieves performance comparable to or better than sparse MoE on language modeling and downstream tasks, while activating significantly fewer experts. Notably, the learned allocation is highly non-uniform, with later layers activating more experts. The source code is publicly available$^\dagger$.
2606.17952.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏專家混合（MoE）架構通過僅激活少量專家來在固定推理預算下擴展LLM參數，這是通過top-$k$路由實現的。雖然這保留了因果關係並適合自回歸語言模型，但離散的top-$k$運算子是不可微分的，這迫使每個輸入有固定數量的活躍專家，導致計算效率低下。我們提出了SoftMoE，它用截斷的軟top-$k$ LapSum放鬆來替代離散路由，允許專家路由的基於梯度的優化。我們進一步參數化每層的平均活躍專家數量並施加全局預算約束，使模型能夠學習如何在各層之間分配專家容量。SoftMoE與自回歸建模完全兼容，並在語言建模和下游任務中達到可比於或優於稀疏MoE的性能，同時激活的專家數量顯著減少。值得注意的是，學習到的分配是高度不均勻的，後面的層激活了更多的專家。源代碼已公開可用$^\dagger$。
2606.17952.author: Mikołaj Zasada et.al.
2606.17952.authors: Mikołaj Zasada, Łukasz Struski, Jacek Tabor, Marcin Kurdziel
